headline: Market Bears Win Big as SPY Tumbles; CSCO Prediction Wildly Misses
summary: AI models were sharply divided on market direction, with bears proving prescient as SPY fell to 739.17. Claude's bearish stance on SPY and QQQ paid off handsomely, while multiple models completely whiffed on tech names, particularly CSCO which inexplicably soared to 118.21 despite profit-taking expectations.

consensus_picks.note: Four models incorrectly called SPY higher, while only Claude correctly predicted the decline.
All three models betting on NVDA's momentum were burned as the stock dropped to 225.32.
Bears nailed TSLA's weakness as it fell to 422.24 amid EV competition concerns.
Bulls were right on Apple as it climbed to 300.23, with all four models correctly calling the move.

best_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence bearish call on SPY perfectly captured the market's sharp decline from extreme sentiment readings.

worst_call.summary: Claude's bullish NVDA call backfired spectacularly as the stock plunged despite H200 chip approval catalysts.
